summary: Implemented fighter material rendering routines in src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c, replacing NOT_IMPLEMENTED bodies for ftMaterial_800BF534 and ftMaterial_800BF6BC and slightly improving ftMaterial_800BF2B8. The decomp.dev report shows ftMaterial_800BF534 became a full match, ftMaterial_800BF6BC improved to 99.47%, and ftMaterial_800BF2B8 improved slightly, adding 392 matched bytes overall.

key_files.role: Only changed file; implemented ftMaterial_800BF534 and ftMaterial_800BF6BC, adjusted ftMaterial_800BF2B8 locals/control flag, and added includes needed for device/lb/debug helpers.

what_changed: Added includes for ft/ftdevice.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, and baselib/debug.h to support fighter material logic, free color register helpers, OSReport, and __assert.

Reworked ftMaterial_800BF2B8 local declarations and scopes, moving variables such as Fighter*, HSD_TObj*, HSD_TObj**, HSD_TExp*, and HSD_PEDesc* into an outer declaration style that better matched the target.
Changed one ftMaterial_800BF2B8 condition from fp->x2223_b4 to fp->x2223_b3 when selecting the HSD_PEDesc path with fp->x61D == 0xFF.

Implemented ftMaterial_800BF534. The function handles a light-enabled ColorOverlay path, reserves a TEV color register with lbGetFreeColorRegister, asserts if unavailable, sets an HSD_TECnst value from overlay->x50_light_color, configures an HSD_TevDesc stage, and returns the provided HSD_TExp when used.

Implemented ftMaterial_800BF6BC. The function computes color overlay state from fighter flags, controller index, metal/dark state, overlay color enablement, fp->x610_color_rgba, and p_ftCommonData->x6D8, then sets TEV constants/registers and configures a TEV stage.
Preserved NOT_IMPLEMENTED removal for the two functions while leaving ftMaterial_800BF6BC just short of full matching according to the bot report.
Bot report: ftMaterial_800BF534 improved from 1.43% to 100.00%; ftMaterial_800BF6BC improved from 0.48% to 99.47%; ftMaterial_800BF2B8 improved from 99.07% to 99.18%.
smart_moves: Used existing global/template data through char* base = (char*) &ftMObj and fixed offsets such as 0x50, 0xC4, 0xF4, 0x118, and 0x128, which is a practical matching tactic when descriptor constants are embedded in a global object but not yet fully structured.
Copied HSD_TECnst and HSD_TevDesc templates before mutating fields, preserving likely original code behavior and improving matchability for HSD TEV setup.
Kept compiler-sensitive temporary names and stack-shaped locals such as sp168, sp18, sp_cnst1, sp_cnst2, and sp_tevdesc, which likely helped reproduce stack layout and register pressure.
Modeled register allocation failure paths with OSReport plus __assert, including distinct assertion line numbers 240, 352, and 366 from the diff.
Maintained exact integer color/alpha math instead of simplifying to more readable formulas, which is important for matching codegen and preserving byte-exact behavior.
Handled the TEV register-selection details with lb_8000CC8C, lb_8000CCA4, lb_8000CD90, and lb_8000CDA8 rather than hardcoding GX constants directly.
decomp_lessons: For fighter material and TEV code, matching often depends on copying descriptor templates from existing global data, then changing only the fields the original code touched.
Do not over-clean local scope in near-matching functions; moving declarations out of nested blocks and preserving temporary variables can improve compiler output.
Color-overlay rendering paths may have several interacting fighter flags: x2223_b2, x2223_b3, x2228_b0, x2224_b0, is_metal, x2227_b3, x61A_controller_index, and x61D all affected this implementation.
Sentinel alpha or material state values such as 0xFF should be preserved explicitly; ftMaterial_800BF6BC uses fp->x61D != 0xFF to decide whether to add an extra TEV constant and alpha selector.

Register allocation helpers should be called with the current material object and any existing expression chain; this patch uses lbGetFreeColorRegister with NULL, texp, or a temporary HSD_TExp chain depending on stage.
When PR evidence is only diff plus bot report, avoid inferring reviewer intent; the only documented outcome here is matching progress.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names were retained: ftMaterial_800BF2B8, ftMaterial_800BF534, ftMaterial_800BF6BC, and ftMaterial_800BFB4C.
Unresolved Fighter fields remained in project-style offset/bitfield naming, such as x2223_b2, x2223_b3, x2228_b0, x2224_b0, x2227_b3, x61D, x61A_controller_index, and x610_color_rgba.
Stack-local names followed decomp matching conventions: sp168, sp100, sp18, sp_cnst1, sp_cnst2, and sp_tevdesc.
Temporary control-flow/register variables used generic compiler-oriented names such as chk, chk1, var_r0, var_r3, var_r5, reg, reg1, and reg2.
Overlay structure naming used existing ColorOverlay fields including x7C_flag2, x7C_light_enable, x7C_color_enable, x50_light_color, and x2C_hex.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED with C that closely mirrors likely assembly, including unusual casts between HSD_TECnst and HSD_TExp for HSD_TExpSetReg and linked next pointers.
Used explicit u8 casts on TEV register numbers and fp->x61D to match original narrow-field behavior.
Preserved boolean/register idioms such as assigning chk = lbGetFreeColorRegister(...) and then reg = chk in ftMaterial_800BF534, even though the resulting code is not idiomatic C.
Used nested small scopes and temporary u8/s32 intermediates in the alpha blending calculation to control code generation.
Configured TEV stages through HSD_StateAssignTev and HSD_SetupTevStage after filling fields like clr_a, clr_b, clr_c, clr_d, kcsel, alpha_d, and kasel.
Maintained exact integer division by 255 and 0xFF arithmetic for overlay color composition rather than using floating-point or simplified blending.

review_feedback.summary: Reported matched code increased to 62.56% (+0.01%, +392 bytes), with one new full match and two unmatched-item improvements.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish ftMaterial_800BF6BC from 99.47% to 100%.
Search for other ftMObj offset-based descriptor template uses to convert into typed struct fields when enough evidence exists.
Compare other fighter material/color overlay implementations for x2223_b2, x2223_b3, x61D, and x610_color_rgba semantics.
Look for subsequent renames of ColorOverlay fields x7C_flag2, x7C_light_enable, x7C_color_enable, x50_light_color, and x2C_hex.
